Title: First-time user registration
Preconditions: Application is launched for the first time.
Test Data: User is not registered.
Test Steps:
1. User launches the application.
2. System prompts the user to register.
3. User completes the registration process.
Expected Result: User is redirected to the HOME state after successful registration.

Title: Existing user login
Preconditions: Application is launched and user is registered.
Test Data: Valid user credentials.
Test Steps:
1. User launches the application.
2. System prompts the user to log in.
3. User enters valid credentials.
4. System verifies credentials with the server.
5. Server confirms credentials with the database.
6. Application receives confirmation from the server.
7. Application informs the user that login was successful.
Expected Result: User is redirected to the HOME state after successful login.

Title: Invalid login attempt
Preconditions: Application is launched and user is registered.
Test Data: Invalid user credentials.
Test Steps:
1. User launches the application.
2. System prompts the user to log in.
3. User enters invalid credentials.
4. Application sends credentials to the server.
5. Server queries the database for verification.
6. Database returns no match.
7. Server informs the application that credentials are invalid.
8. Application informs the user that login failed.
Expected Result: User remains on the login screen with an error message.

Title: Start a new trip
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the HOME state.
Test Data: User wants to start a trip.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the HOME state.
2. User selects to start a new trip.
3. System transitions to the Tragitto in corso state.
Expected Result: User is now in the Tragitto in corso state.

Title: Complete a trip and calculate emissions
Preconditions: User is logged in and has an ongoing trip.
Test Data: User completes a trip.
Test Steps:
1. User is in the Tragitto in corso state.
2. User marks the trip as completed.
3. System transitions to the Tragitto terminato state.
4. System transitions to the Calcolo emissioni state.
5. Server calculates CO2 emissions based on the trip data.
6. Application displays the calculated emissions.
Expected Result: User sees the calculated CO2 emissions for the completed trip.

Title: Return to HOME state without changing section
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the HOME state.
Test Data: User does not want to change section.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the HOME state.
2. User selects not to change section.
3. System remains in the HOME state.
Expected Result: User remains on the HOME state.

Title: View weekly report
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the Statistiche state.
Test Data: User wants to view a weekly report.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the Statistiche state.
2. User selects to view a weekly report.
3. System transitions to the Report Settimanale state.
4. Application displays the weekly report.
Expected Result: User sees the weekly report.

Title: Return to Statistiche state without viewing report
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the Statistiche state.
Test Data: User does not want to view a weekly report.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the Statistiche state.
2. User selects not to view a weekly report.
3. System remains in the Statistiche state.
Expected Result: User remains on the Statistiche state.

Title: View personal data
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the Profilo state.
Test Data: User wants to view personal data.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the Profilo state.
2. User selects to view personal data.
3. System remains in the Profilo state.
Expected Result: User sees their personal data.

Title: Update account details
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the Profilo state.
Test Data: User wants to update account details.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the Profilo state.
2. User selects to update account details.
3. User inputs new data.
4. Application sends updated data to the server.
5. Server updates the database.
6. Application confirms data update.
Expected Result: User sees a confirmation message indicating that their account details have been updated.

Title: Logout from the application
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the Profilo state.
Test Data: User wants to logout.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the Profilo state.
2. User selects to logout.
3. Application sends logout request to the server.
4. Server logs out the user.
5. Application redirects the user to the START state.
Expected Result: User is redirected to the START state.

Title: Change section within the application
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the Profilo state.
Test Data: User wants to change section.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the Profilo state.
2. User selects to change section.
3. System transitions to the Menu Orizzontale state.
Expected Result: User sees the Menu Orizzontale state.

Title: Successful login sequence
Preconditions: User is attempting to log in.
Test Data: Valid user credentials.
Test Steps:
1. User provides valid credentials to the application.
2. Application sends credentials to the server.
3. Server queries the database for verification.
4. Database confirms credentials.
5. Server sends confirmation to the application.
6. Application confirms login success to the user.
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the HOME state.

Title: Failed login sequence due to invalid credentials
Preconditions: User is attempting to log in.
Test Data: Invalid user credentials.
Test Steps:
1. User provides invalid credentials to the application.
2. Application sends credentials to the server.
3. Server queries the database for verification.
4. Database returns no match.
5. Server informs the application that credentials are invalid.
6. Application informs the user that login failed.
Expected Result: User sees an error message indicating login failure.

Title: Calculate CO2 emissions for a trip
Preconditions: User is logged in and has initiated a trip.
Test Data: User completes a trip.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates a trip.
2. Application sends trip data to the server.
3. Server stores the trip data in the database.
4. Server calculates CO2 emissions based on the trip data.
5. Server sends the calculated emissions back to the application.
6. Application displays the calculated emissions to the user.
Expected Result: User sees the calculated CO2 emissions for the completed trip.

Title: Store trip data in the database
Preconditions: User is logged in and has initiated a trip.
Test Data: User completes a trip.
Test Steps:
1. User initiates a trip.
2. Application sends trip data to the server.
3. Server stores the trip data in the database.
4. Server confirms the storage of trip data.
Expected Result: Server confirms that the trip data has been stored in the database.

Title: Display calculated emissions to the user
Preconditions: User is logged in and has completed a trip.
Test Data: Calculated CO2 emissions.
Test Steps:
1. User completes a trip.
2. Server calculates CO2 emissions based on the trip data.
3. Server sends the calculated emissions back to the application.
4. Application displays the calculated emissions to the user.
Expected Result: User sees the calculated CO2 emissions for the completed trip.

2. User selects to update account details.
3. User cancels the update process.
4. System remains in the Profilo state.
Expected Result: User remains on the Profilo state without any changes made.
